title: A Multimodal Neuroimaging Dataset to Study Spatiotemporal Dynamics of Visual Processing in Humans
description: A multimodal neuroimaging dataset designed to investigate the spatiotemporal dynamics of visual processing in humans. The dataset combines EEG, structural MRI (anat), and functional MRI (func) modalities to characterize neural responses during visual tasks. Participants performed visual perception tasks while simultaneous EEG and fMRI recordings were acquired, with structural MRI scans obtained for anatomical reference. This resource enables investigation of the temporal and spatial organization of visual cortical processing across multiple neuroimaging modalities. This dataset is version 1.3.0 of OpenNeuro dataset ds002814.
methods_description: Participants performed visual perception tasks during simultaneous EEG and fMRI acquisition. Structural MRI scans were obtained for anatomical reference and co-registration. EEG was recorded using a standard cap with electrodes positioned according to the 10-20 system. fMRI data were acquired using a 3T scanner with standard echo-planar imaging sequences. Visual stimuli were presented on a screen visible to participants in the scanner. Data were preprocessed according to standard neuroimaging protocols and are provided in BIDS format.
